Hey! 👋 How's it going?

I need you to do

TXT 50.62 KB
Muslim Assalamu alaikum. My name's Mustafa, I'm 22, based in Canada. I landed my first high-ticket sales role in just 5 days flat, no experience — because I don't do anything halfway. Before that, I built a YouTube channel from absolute scratch — no audience, just months of daily consistency until I hit over 7 million stream views and 8,000+ subscribers. That's exactly why I invested in a mentorship program instead of waiting around — I saw high-ticket sales as my future, put in the training, hit the deadlines and targets I'd sharpened through studying finance and marketing, and landed a role in 5 days flat. I've spent 2 and a half years in customer service — handling complaints, holding my frame under pressure, and actually understanding a person's problem before I ever try to solve it. That's what setting really is: finding the prospect's pain and tailoring the path forward, not just booking a call. In my first role, I treated the business like my own — recording VSLs they were about to outsource, tripling the outreach target, and keeping CRM hygiene spotless in real time. Give me your ICP and your systems, and I'll skip the week of ramp-up — I'll be ready in a day. Appointment setting is my next goal, and I'm giving it the same commitment that built a channel from zero to 7 million views. So what are we waiting for? Let's get in touch. I also uploaded my current best script, tweak it and improve it to the max Here are some important advices I got from a survey of friends: Loom checklist Confidence in the voice (make sure to practice script many times) Looking at the camera Strong hook (talk about your very best accomplishment at the very start) Mention tangible skills that you can apply in sales (wrestling, uni, hard working, For muslim brands talk about seeking knowledge consistently + benefitting ummah Do it on notion/presentation Say you will be looking back and saying this was the best decision Availible, Always shows up first and leaves last Outworked all other setters Invested in a high ticket sales program for remoterizq Left job for remoterizq Top 1 clash (talk abt consistency)- Detailing cars, Anwar Apparel, investing, etc (Im always willing to take risks) Good at tracking in CRM Networking in many communities Genuine conversations (2.5 years of customer experience) Coachable (Listen, Adapt, Execute) Mention Wrestling Looking for a professional home (trying to elevate, will treat business like its mine) Say HERES THE PATTERN High standards (treat it likeit’s yours) Give them a either or Intro/Fact Personal Introduction (name, age etc.) Proof in the pudding Landed my first role in 5 days after investing in a mentorship program called Remote Rizq Coachability from wrestling Studied Finance and Marketing + seeking ilm helped me become disciplined Invested in myself; had a clothing brand, car detailing, ex-pro player on Clash Royale, youtuber with 100ks of views which all taught me resilience Genuine conversations 2.5 years of customer experience (27 min) able to understand the customers needs as setting is all about knowing where a prospect is that, where they want to go, and qualifying them to see if we can even help. Say how just give me your ICP + teach me about ur syustems and ill be done the onboarding in only 1 day Mention how u left job cuz high ticket sales is ur future n how u go all in Mention customer service experience Mention investments and why I invested, why i see it as my future, how I grew in it, I did training, deadlines, targets, landed role 5 days later I also treated their business like my business, and actually like a business owner, by taking on extra responsibilities by doing XYZ. Maybe include studying as it shows discipline Include everything abt ICP and systems, ill start in a day not a week Talked to customers under pressure UNDERSTANDING THEIR NEEDS (apt setting is abt understanding prospects pain) Handling complaints, conversations under pressure Upholding my frame of authority Yeah. Understanding the person's problems and tailoring a solution to that problem. For closing, seem more serious For apt setting, be more friendly and genuine Talk about how I achieved the goals (how many applications) Say how apt setting is my new goal n ill be committing fully to it just like I commited to my channel and showed previous commitment in previous endavours Focus a lot of channel and how I started a channel from scratch after being consistent for months straight n attained 7 mil total stream views and 8k+ subs SUMMARY OF ADVICE (focus especially on getting role in 5 days, 2.5 years of customer experience, and youtube streaming being consistent for months) Here is a summary of my skills: id say focus on the 3 in the summary: Seeking Islamic knowledge for over a year (SOK Academy) Studied Finance and Marketing at the University of Ottawa Lead generation internship at PolyPlastics Marketing intern at ContentBlocks Car detailing A clothing brand Investing in the stock market (500% return on best stock) Ex-pro player for popular strategy game Clash Royale (Peaked top 1 in Canada) YouTube streamer build channel from scratch (7 mil total views on streams and 8k subs in 5 months) Wrestling for over a year I can finish your trainings in 1/5th of the time Made presentations and recorded VSLs for Platera Tripled suggested outreach (worked harder than anyone else) Maintained CRM hygiene (updated in real time) 2.5 years of customer experience (mainly Walmart and Costco) Volunteered at multiple schools (1 year) Invested in a high-ticket sales mentorship program (Remote Rizq) but just incase u could maybe use something super super strong for appointment setting roles
Edit
Copy
Share
I need to craft a 1.5-minute max Loom script for appointment setting positions targeting Muslim businesses, incorporating all your coach's feedback, your skills, and your friends' advice. Let me break down the key strategic elements:
Core Pattern-Breaking Strategy:
  • Open with your strongest hook (5-day role, not your backstory)
  • Lead with proof, not potential
  • Make every sentence earn its place
  • End with a direct call-to-action (the "either/or" close)

THE SCRIPT — ~1:30 when delivered with energy

[0:00-0:06] — HOOK (Break the pattern immediately)
"I landed my first high-ticket sales role in 5 days flat — zero experience, one mentorship program, and a decision to go all in. I'm Mustafa, 22, based in Canada, and I'm not here to tell you what I might do. I'm here to show you what I already do."
[0:06-0:28] — THE PROOF (Tangible, not theoretical)
"I built a YouTube channel from absolute scratch — no audience, no connections, just months of showing up every single day until I hit over 7 million stream views and 8,000 subscribers. That's not luck. That's obsession. I don't dabble. I go all in, and I become top 1% at it. Same with Clash Royale — peaked top 1 in Canada. Same with wrestling — a year of showing up when it hurt. Same with this."
[0:28-0:50] — WHY THIS MATTERS FOR YOU (Link to appointment setting)
"Here's the pattern: I obsess, I commit, I execute. In my first role at Platera, I finished onboarding before anyone else, tripled the outreach target, recorded VSLs they were about to outsource, and kept CRM data spotless in real time. I treated their business like mine. Because setting isn't just booking calls — it's understanding someone's pain, holding frame under pressure, and qualifying whether we can actually help them transform. That's what 2 and a half years of customer service at Walmart and Costco taught me: people don't care what you know until they know you understand where they are."
[0:50-1:08] — THE OFFER (What you bring, not what you want)
"I've studied finance and marketing, sought ilm for over a year at SOK Academy, and invested in Remote Rizq because high-ticket sales is my future — not a side hustle, not a maybe. Give me your ICP, your systems, and one day. Not a week. One day. I'll be ramped and ready. Skill can be taught. But consistency, resilience, and treating your business like my own? Those are traits you can't install in someone."
[1:08-1:25] — THE CLOSE (Direct, confident, either/or)
"The Muslim market is growing fast, and the only way to capture that growth is revenue. I control the pipeline. I show up first, I leave last, and I outwork. So here's my question: are we setting up a call this week, or are we setting up a call next week? Either way, insha'Allah — let's not waste time."
[1:25-1:30] — SIGN-OFF
"JazakAllahu khair. Talk soon."

Delivery Notes:

Table
Copy
ElementHow to Execute
Eye contactLook directly into the camera lens, not the screen
EnergyStart at 8/10 energy and hold it — this is your life, not a presentation
ToneFriendly but serious. You're a peer, not a beggar
PacingPause slightly after "5 days flat" and "7 million stream views" — let it land
BackgroundClean, professional, good lighting, mic visible
Notion/PresentationUse a simple slide with your name + key stat visible

What I Cut & Why:

Table
Copy
CutReason
Metro minimum wage backstoryWeakens the hook — lead with win, not loss
"Man on a mission" / "proof is in the pudding"Generic filler every other candidate says
List of every hustleCluttered; YouTube + Clash Royale + wrestling cover the pattern
Clothing brand / car detailing / stocksDilutes focus; keep if you need to pad, but not in 1.5 min
"Professional home" phraseOverused in sales scripts
"Benefiting the ummah" rhetoricLet your actions and targeting speak for it; show, don't tell

What I Kept & Amplified:

Table
Copy
KeptWhy It Hits
5-day rolePattern-breaker — most people take months
YouTube 7M views / 8K subs / daily consistencyDemonstrates organic growth, discipline, communication skills
Top 1 Canada Clash RoyaleCompetitive drive + strategic thinking
WrestlingCoachability, pain tolerance, discipline
Tripled outreach / VSLs / CRM hygieneProof you already operate like an owner
2.5 years customer serviceDirectly maps to prospect pain + qualification
"One day, not one week" onboardingSpecific, confident, memorable
Either/or closeForces decision, shows confidence

Final Polish — The "Pattern Break" Check:

"Most Looms say: 'I'm hardworking, coachable, and resilient.' This script says: 'I hit 7 million views from zero, top 1 in Canada, and landed a role in 5 days — and I'm about to do the same for your pipeline.'"
That's the difference between telling and demonstrating.
Want me to also draft a non-Muslim version (secular tone, no Islamic references) or a personalized template where you drop in the company name + one specific detail about their offer?

I landed my first high-ticket sales role in 5 days flat — zero experience, one mentorship program called Remote Rizq, and a decision to go all in. I'm Mustafa, 22, based in Canada, and I'm not here to tell you what I might do — I'm here to show you what I already do. I built a YouTube channel from absolute scratch — no audience, no connections, just months of showing up every single day until I hit over 7 million stream views and 8,000 subscribers. That's not luck. That's obsession. I don't dabble — I go all in, and I become top 1% at it. Same with Clash Royale where I peaked top 1 in Canada. Same with wrestling for over a year — showing up when it hurt. Same with this. Here's the pattern: I obsess, I commit, I execute. In my first role at Platera, I finished onboarding before anyone else, tripled the outreach target, recorded VSLs they were about to outsource, and kept CRM data spotless in real time. I treated their business like mine. Because setting isn't just booking calls — it's understanding someone's pain, holding frame under pressure, and qualifying whether we can actually help them transform. That's what 2 and a half years of customer service at Walmart and Costco taught me: people don't care what you know until they know you understand where they are. I've studied finance and marketing, sought ilm for over a year at SOK Academy, and invested in Remote Rizq because high-ticket sales is my future — not a side hustle, not a maybe. Give me your ICP, your systems, and one day. Not a week. One day. I'll be ramped and ready. Skill can be taught. But consistency, resilience, and treating your business like my own? Those are traits you can't install in someone. The Muslim market is growing fast, and the only way to capture that growth is revenue. I control the pipeline. I show up first, I leave last, and I outwork. So here's my question: are we setting up a call this week, or are we setting up a call next week? Either way, insha'Allah — let's not waste time. JazakAllahu khair. Talk soon.

I'll create a visually stunning, interactive widget that you can use as your presentation board — think of it as a digital "skill dashboard" you can screen-share during your Loom or live calls. It will categorize your skills into 4 powerful pillars and present them in a clean, professional layout.
Let me build this for you now.
Here's your interactive Skill Dashboard — a 4-pillar layout you can screen-share during Looms or live calls. Each pillar maps directly to what hiring managers actually care about in appointment setting:
Table
Copy
PillarWhy It Matters for Setting
🎯 CoachabilityThey need someone who can absorb feedback fast and adapt — your wrestling + mentorship + ilm prove this
🔥 ConsistencySetting is a volume game. Your YouTube daily streak + Clash Royale grind + tripling outreach prove you don't quit
🚀 Ownership MindsetThey want someone who treats the business like theirs — your VSLs, CRM hygiene, side hustles, and leaving Metro for this
🤝 Communication & EmpathySetting is pain-first, not pitch-first — your 2.5 years customer service + streaming + qualifying philosophy

How to Use This in Your Loom:

  1. Screen-share this dashboard as your background while you deliver your script
  2. Point to specific pillars as you mention them (e.g., "Coachability — I wrestled for a year, I know how to take feedback under pressure")
  3. Let it do the heavy lifting — your script covers the highlights, the board fills in the gaps

Want Me to Also Build You:

  • A Canva-style static image (PNG) with the same layout — for attaching to emails or LinkedIn messages
  • A "One-Pager" version — all 4 pillars condensed into a single vertical image for mobile viewing
  • A personalized version where you drop in a company logo + one tailored skill per pillar
Just say the word — or tell me if you want to tweak colors, add/remove skills, or rename any pillars!
I love it, but is there a way I can upload this or code it or get a link on a website? or maybe you can code it for me somehow and make a new website? Or maybe a canva link, are u able to access my canva and make this there? let me know
Edit
Copy
Share
I can't access your Canva account or create accounts on external platforms for you, but I can give you several ways to get this live and shareable. Here are your best options, ranked by ease:

Option 1: GitHub Pages (FREE, Permanent Link, Easiest)

I'll generate the full HTML file for you. You upload it to GitHub, and it becomes a live website in ~2 minutes.
Steps:
  1. Go to github.com → create a free account
  2. Create a new repo called mustafa-skills (make it public)
  3. Upload the HTML file I'll give you
  4. Go to Settings → Pages → Select "main branch" → Save
  5. You'll get a link like https://yourusername.github.io/mustafa-skills
Pros: Free forever, custom domain possible, looks professional, easy to update

Option 2: CodePen (FREE, Instant, No Setup)

I can give you the HTML/CSS code. You paste it into codepen.io, and you get a shareable link immediately.
Pros: Zero setup, instant link, can edit visually Cons: CodePen branding on free tier, less "professional" than a real site

Option 3: Netlify Drop (FREE, Drag & Drop)

Go to netlify.com → "Add new site" → "Deploy manually" → drag your HTML file in. Done. Live link in seconds.

Option 4: Canva (Manual Rebuild)

I can't access your Canva, but I can give you the exact layout specs — colors, fonts, spacing, element positions — so you can rebuild it pixel-perfect in ~15 minutes. Or you can screenshot the widget I made and use that as a background image in Canva, then add text boxes on top.

My Recommendation:

Go with Option 1 (GitHub Pages). It gives you a clean, permanent URL you can put in your email signature, LinkedIn, and Loom descriptions. It screams "this guy knows what he's doing."
Want me to generate the complete HTML file right now so you can upload it? I can also add a few extras:
  • Your photo/headshot
  • A "Book a Call" button
  • Your LinkedIn / email links
  • A subtle animation on hover
Just tell me which option you want and if you want any extras added!
I just made a github account, where do I go from here?
Edit
Copy
Share
Perfect — let's get your site live in the next few minutes. Follow these steps exactly:

Step 1: Create the Repository

  1. Go to github.com and log in
  2. Click the "+" icon (top right) → "New repository"
  3. Name it: mustafa-skills (exactly like that, no spaces)
  4. Make sure "Public" is selected
  5. Click "Create repository" (green button at the bottom)

Step 2: Upload the HTML File

Once your repo is created:
  1. You'll see a page that says "Quick setup — if you've done this kind of thing before"
  2. Click "uploading an existing file" (the blue link)
  3. Drag and drop the file I'll give you, OR click "choose your files"
  4. Scroll down, type this in the "Commit changes" box: Add skills dashboard
  5. Click "Commit changes" (green button)

Step 3: Enable GitHub Pages

  1. In your repo, click "Settings" (top tab, next to "Insights")
  2. In the left sidebar, click "Pages" (under "Code and automation")
  3. Under "Source", select "Deploy from a branch"
  4. Under "Branch", select "main" and folder "/ (root)"
  5. Click "Save"
  6. Wait ~1-2 minutes, then refresh the page
  7. You'll see a green box at the top with your live link: https://yourusername.github.io/mustafa-skills

Right Now — Here's Your File:

I'm generating the complete index.html file. Save it to your computer as index.html (make sure it ends in .html, not .txt).
Let me build it with a few extras — your contact info, a "Book a Call" button, and smooth animations.
Execute Python code
Your index.html file is ready! Here's your download link:
Download index.html

Quick Steps to Go Live:

1. Download the file

Click the link above → it will download index.html to your computer.

2. Upload to GitHub

  • Go to your mustafa-skills repo on GitHub
  • Click "Add file" → "Upload files"
  • Drag index.html into the box (or click "choose your files")
  • Scroll down, type: Add skills dashboard
  • Click "Commit changes"

3. Enable Pages (if you haven't already)

  • In your repo, click Settings → Pages (left sidebar)
  • Under Source, select "Deploy from a branch"
  • Branch: main / Folder: /(root)
  • Click Save
  • Wait ~1-2 minutes, refresh — you'll see your live link: https://YOURUSERNAME.github.io/mustafa-skills

FeatureWhy It Helps
"Book a Call" buttonDirect CTA — they can book while viewing your skills
Contact links (email, LinkedIn, Calendly)Multiple touchpoints, looks professional
Smooth hover animationsFeels premium, memorable
Fade-in animations on loadCinematic first impression
Mobile responsiveLooks clean on phone too
Closing taglineReinforces your work ethic

I love the formatting, is there a way you can allow me to edit it? or would you prefer if I just list the things I need to change and you can do them for me?
Edit
Copy
Share
Both work, but since you might want to tweak things on the fly (new skills, different wording, updated stats), let me give you both options:

Option 1: Easy Self-Editing (Recommended)

The file is just HTML/CSS — you can edit it directly on GitHub after uploading, no coding knowledge needed:
  1. Go to your repo on GitHub
  2. Click index.html
  3. Click the pencil icon (top right) to edit
  4. Change any text you want
  5. Scroll down, type what you changed, click "Commit changes"
  6. The site updates automatically in ~30 seconds
What you can safely change: Any text inside quotes — names, numbers, descriptions, links. Just don't touch the < or > symbols or the CSS styling sections.

Option 2: I Edit It For You

Just list what you want changed and I'll regenerate the file. Much easier if you have a lot of edits.
Which do you prefer? If you want me to handle it, just paste your changes here — even rough notes work. For example:
  • "Change email to mustafa.walwil@gmail.com"
  • "Add my Calendly link: calendly.com/mustafawalwil"
  • "Remove the volunteering bullet under Consistency"
  • "Change '7M+ views' to '8M+ views'"
I'll update everything and give you the new file.
before I tell you what to edit, just a quick question, on github, can anyone open the link or do they have to have an account? I dont want employers to be lazy and not make an account and ignore me. when we finihs making this, is it just an easy link and they click and it opens everything perfectly? how will it go?
Edit
Copy
Share
Great question — and yes, you're overthinking it. Here's the reality:
Anyone can open your GitHub Pages link. Zero account needed. It's just a normal website link like https://mustafawalwil.github.io/mustafa-skills. They click it, it opens instantly in their browser — no login, no signup, no friction.
Think of it like this: GitHub hosts the file, but GitHub Pages turns it into a standalone website. The person clicking has no idea it's on GitHub. It looks and works like any professional portfolio site.
What they'll see:
  • Clean URL in your email/Loom description
  • Click → page loads in 1-2 seconds
  • Full dashboard with your skills, contact info, book-a-call button
  • Works on desktop, mobile, tablet
What they WON'T see:
  • GitHub branding
  • Login prompts
  • Any indication it was built on GitHub
So employers won't even know GitHub is involved. To them, it's just a slick personal site.
Now — hit me with those edits whenever you're ready! 🎯
